subject: Human Resources
title: The Hawthorne Effect
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Hawthorne Effect.

hook.text: Researchers changed the lighting in a factory. Productivity went up. They changed it back. Productivity still went up. What?

buildup.text: The Hawthorne studies (1924-1932) at Western Electric found that workers performed better simply because they were being observed.

discovery.text: The Hawthorne Effect: people change behavior when observed—attention itself improves performance temporarily.

twist.text: Modern scholars debate whether the original results were valid—the data was messy and the conclusions oversimplified.

climax.text: Despite the controversy, the core lesson endures: paying attention to workers matters. People perform better when they feel seen.

punchline.text: Sometimes the intervention doesn't matter. The attention does.

quiz.question: What is the Hawthorne Effect?
options: People modify behavior when they know they're being observed
